ChamaPro — Official User Guide
Complete documentation for members and administrators
 Admin Guide Included
Official Documentation
Welcome to ChamaPro
Your complete guide to managing chamas, contributions, loans, fines, M-Pesa payments, and more. Designed for both members and administrators.
Members & Admins M-Pesa Integrated Role-Based Access Reports & Exports E-Wallet
Built by Dantechdevs · Kenya  🇰🇪 · Django 5.1
Table of Contents
Overview User Roles & Permissions Sign Up & Login Dashboard Your Profile & KYC Contributions Loans Fines & Penalties M-Pesa Payments E-Wallet Messaging Creating a Chama (Admin) Managing Members (Admin) Chama Settings (Admin) Delete & Restore (Admin) Reports & Exports (Admin) Investments (Admin) Pricing Plans URL Reference Data Models M-Pesa Integration Glossary
Introduction

What is ChamaPro?

ChamaPro is a web-based chama management platform built for Kenyan savings groups. It helps chamas track contributions, manage loans, issue fines, send M-Pesa payment requests, export financial reports, and communicate with members — all in one place.

Contributions

Record and track member contributions by cash, M-Pesa, bank transfer, or cheque with full history and filtering.

Loans

Apply for loans, approve them as admin, and track repayments with automatic interest calculation.

Fines

Issue and manage fines for late contributions, missed meetings, or other violations. Members pay via M-Pesa.

M-Pesa STK Push

Send payment requests directly to members' phones. Payments are automatically recorded when confirmed.

Reports

Generate financial summaries and export as PDF, Excel, or CSV for record-keeping and audits.

E-Wallet

Each member and the group has a digital wallet for tracking funds, deposits, and withdrawals.

Access Control

User Roles & Permissions

ChamaPro uses role-based access control. Every member has a role within each chama that determines what they can see and do.

Feature Admin Treasurer Secretary Member
View dashboard
Record contributions
Approve / reject loans
Apply for a loan
Issue fines
Send M-Pesa STK push
Invite / remove members
Change member roles
Edit chama settings
Archive / delete chama
Download reports (PDF/Excel)
View investments
One admin minimum A chama must always have at least one admin. You cannot demote yourself if you are the only admin — promote another member first.
Authentication

Sign Up & Login

Creating an account takes less than a minute. You can log in with your email, phone number, or username.

Creating an Account
One-time registration
1

Go to Sign Up

Visit /signup/ and fill in your full name, email address, phone number, and a password (minimum 6 characters).

2

Submit the form

Your account is created immediately — no email verification required. You are logged in automatically.

3

Create or join a chama

Once logged in you can create your own chama or wait for an admin to invite you to an existing one. You can be a member of multiple chamas at the same time.

Login options You can log in using your email address, phone number (e.g. 0712345678), or your auto-generated username. Forgot your password? Visit /forgot-password/.
Main Interface

Dashboard

The dashboard is your home screen. It shows your active chama's financial summary, member balances, quick access links, and upcoming meetings.

Dashboard — Main view: stat cards, group information panel, balances table
Dashboard — Quick Access grid: Members, Contributions, M-Pesa, Loans, Reports, PDF, Excel, Profile, New Chama, Logout
Dashboard Sections
1

Stat Cards

Shows Total Contributions, Outstanding Loans, Active Members, and Next Meeting at a glance.

2

Group Information Panel

Displays cash at bank, total contributions, outstanding loans, and a 12-month trend chart.

3

Balances Table

Lists all members with their contribution paid amount and arrears. Click View all for the full contributions page.

4

Quick Access Grid

One-click shortcuts to Members, Contributions, M-Pesa, Loans, Reports, PDF/Excel exports, My Profile, New Chama, and Logout.

5

Upcoming Meetings

Any scheduled meetings appear here. Admins can schedule from the Settings page.

Switching Chamas If you belong to multiple chamas, use the chama selector in the sidebar to switch between them. Your session remembers the last active chama.
Account

Your Profile & KYC

Your profile holds your personal details, payment information, notification preferences, KYC verification status, credit score, and a full view of your chama memberships.

My Profile — Daniel Ngwasi · 2 Chamas · KYC Unverified · Credit Score 750/850 Excellent · My Chamas panel
Profile — Credit score breakdown · Personal Details: National ID, KRA PIN, M-Pesa No., Bank, Occupation, Location, KYC Status Unverified
Profile — Notifications & Preferences: M-Pesa alerts On, SMS On, Email Weekly, 2FA Enabled · Contribution Trend 6 months
Profile Sections
1

Header Stats

Total contributed, active loan balance, outstanding fines, and payment rate — all at a glance across all chamas.

2

Credit Score

A score from 0–850 based on contribution consistency, outstanding fines, and active chamas. A higher score improves loan eligibility.

3

Personal Details

National ID, KRA PIN, M-Pesa number, bank, occupation, and location. Click Edit → to update.

4

Notifications & Preferences

Toggle M-Pesa alerts, SMS reminders, email reports (frequency), and Two-Factor Auth.

5

My Chamas & Contribution Trend

View all your active chamas with your role and contribution amount, plus a 6-month bar chart of your contributions.

KYC Verification
Know Your Customer
1

Add National ID and KRA PIN

Go to Edit Profile → KYC & Identity section and fill in your National ID number and KRA PIN.

2

Submit KYC

Click the orange Submit KYC button on your profile page. Your status changes to Pending Review.

3

Await verification

Once verified, your status shows Verified with a green badge and your credit score improves.

Financial

Contributions

Contributions are the regular savings members make to the chama. Admins and treasurers record contributions; all members can view them.

Contributions — Total Confirmed KES 10,000 · Pending KES 0 · This Month KES 10,000
Recording a Contribution
Admin / Treasurer only
1

Open Contributions

Navigate via sidebar → Contributions or /chama/<id>/contributions/.

2

Click + Record Contribution

Select the member, enter the amount, choose a payment method (M-Pesa, Cash, Bank, Cheque), and optionally add a reference number and notes.

3

Set status

Choose Confirmed, Pending, or Rejected. Confirmed contributions count in financial summaries. M-Pesa contributions via STK Push are auto-confirmed.

Financial

Loans

Members can apply for loans from the chama pool. Admins and treasurers review applications, approve or reject them, and record repayments.

Loans — Total Disbursed, Outstanding, Pending Approval, Overdue counters · All Loans table
Applying for a Loan
All members
1

Open Loans

Navigate via sidebar → Loans and click + New Loan Application or Apply for First Loan.

2

Fill the application

Enter amount, interest rate, repayment term (months), purpose, and description.

3

Wait for approval

Loan status shows as Pending Approval. An admin or treasurer will review and approve or reject it.

4

Repay your loan

Make repayments via M-Pesa STK Push or have a treasurer record cash/bank payments. The system tracks your remaining balance automatically.

Interest Calculation Interest = Loan Amount × Interest Rate ÷ 100. Total Payable = Loan Amount + Interest. The default chama loan interest rate is set in Chama Settings (default 10%).
Overdue Loans If your due date passes without full repayment, the loan status changes to Overdue. Contact your chama admin to discuss repayment options.
Compliance

Fines & Penalties

Admins and treasurers can issue fines to members for late contributions, missed meetings, or other violations. Members can pay fines via M-Pesa.

Fines & Penalties — Total Unpaid / Paid / Waived / All Fines · All Fines table
Fine Types
ReasonDescription
late_contributionContribution received after the due date
missed_contributionContribution not made at all for a period
meeting_absenceMember was absent from a scheduled meeting
otherAny other reason specified by the admin
Payments

M-Pesa Payments

ChamaPro integrates with Safaricom's Daraja API to send STK Push payment requests directly to members' phones. No manual transfers needed.

M-Pesa Payments — Lipa na M-Pesa STK Push · Send STK Push form · Transaction History
How STK Push Works
1

Admin initiates a push

Enter the member's M-Pesa number (format 254XXXXXXXXX), amount, and select payment type (Contribution or Loan Repayment).

2

Member receives a prompt

The member's phone shows a payment prompt with the chama name and amount.

3

Member enters their PIN

Payment is processed through M-Pesa and the receipt number is returned.

4

Auto-recorded in ChamaPro

A contribution or loan repayment is automatically created with the M-Pesa receipt as reference. Status is set to Confirmed.

Finance

E-Wallet

ChamaPro provides two wallet types: a personal E-Wallet for each member and a Group Wallet for the chama's pooled funds.

E-Wallet — Personal wallet: available balance, top up, transfer, withdraw, transaction history
Group Wallet (Admin view) — Group balance, pending withdrawals, member wallet balances
Deposits — Top Up via M-Pesa STK Push · wallet balance · deposit history
Deposits — Record Cash / Bank Deposit form
Withdrawals — Request Withdrawal form · Withdrawal History

Personal Wallet

Top up via M-Pesa STK Push or record cash/bank deposit. Transfer funds to the group. Request withdrawals (admin approval required). Full transaction history.

Group Wallet (Admin)

View group balance, total received, and total disbursed. Review and approve or reject member withdrawal requests. See all member wallet balances.

Depositing to Your Wallet
1

Top Up via M-Pesa

Go to E-Wallet → Deposits. Enter your phone number and amount, then click Send STK Push. An M-Pesa prompt will appear on your phone.

2

Record Cash / Bank Deposit

Select source (Cash, Bank), enter amount and reference number, optionally upload proof, and click Submit for Approval. A treasurer confirms and credits your wallet.

Withdrawals require approval Withdrawal requests are reserved immediately and returned to your balance if rejected by the admin. Approved withdrawals are paid out to your registered M-Pesa number.
Communication

Messaging Hub

Send announcements, reminders, and messages to all chama members or specific individuals directly from ChamaPro.

Messaging Hub — Emails, SMS, Notifications, Announcements · Recent Notifications

Emails

Send internal emails to individual members or the whole group.

SMS

Send SMS messages directly to member phone numbers.

Notifications

In-app alerts and updates visible to members when they log in.

Announcements

Broadcast messages to all chama members at once.

Admin — Getting Started

Creating a Chama

Any registered user can create a chama. The creator automatically becomes the Admin.

Steps to Create a Chama
1

Click New Chama

From the dashboard Quick Access grid or the sidebar chama selector dropdown → New Chama.

2

Fill in chama details

Name (required), description, monthly contribution amount, contribution day (1–28), meeting day, and currency (KES, UGX, TZS, USD).

3

Submit

The chama is created and becomes your active chama. You are automatically added as Admin. Start inviting members right away.

Admin

Managing Members

Admins can invite new members, assign roles, and remove members who are no longer part of the chama.

Members — Invite a Member · Members list with role, phone, join date
Inviting a Member
1

Go to Members

Navigate via sidebar → Members.

2

Enter member's email and role

The person must already have a ChamaPro account. Enter their email, select a role (Member, Secretary, Treasurer, Admin), and click + Add Member.

3

Member is added immediately

They see the chama next time they log in — no invitation email required.

Removing a Member Removing a member sets their membership to inactive. Their historical contributions, loans, and fines are preserved. You cannot remove yourself.
Admin

Chama Settings

Only admins can access chama settings. Settings are organized into tabs — Chama, Roles & Permissions, Notifications, Profile, and Billing.

Settings — Chama tab: Group Identity (name, description, Chama ID, status Active since May 03 2026)
Settings — Schedule & Contributions: meeting Mon, contribution KES 1,000, due 10th, max 10 members, late penalty 5%, loan interest 10%
Settings — Currency (KES / UGX / TZS / USD) · Danger Zone: Transfer Admin, Archive Chama, Delete Chama
Settings — Danger Zone: Archive Chama (freeze activity) · Delete Chama (permanent, cannot be undone)
Settings Sections
SectionWhat you can change
Group IdentityChama name and description
Schedule & ContributionsMeeting day, contribution amount, due day of month, max members, late penalty %, loan interest rate %
CurrencyKES (Kenyan Shilling), UGX (Ugandan Shilling), TZS (Tanzanian Shilling), or USD
Danger ZoneTransfer Admin, Archive Chama (freeze activity), Delete Chama (permanent)
Danger Zone actions Archive freezes all activity but preserves data — members cannot transact. Delete permanently removes the chama and all its data and cannot be undone. Transfer Admin hands admin privileges to another member.
Admin

Delete & Restore a Chama

ChamaPro supports both soft archiving (freeze activity) and hard deletion. Use Archive to pause a chama safely, or Delete to permanently remove it.

Archive

Freezes all activity. Data is preserved but members cannot make contributions, loans, or any transactions. Reversible.

Delete

Permanently deletes the chama and all its data. This cannot be undone. Use with extreme caution.

Transfer Admin instead If you need to step back from managing the chama, use Transfer Admin in the Danger Zone to hand over admin privileges to another trusted member.
Admin

Reports & Analytics

Generate financial summaries and export them in multiple formats for record-keeping, auditing, or sharing with chama members.

Reports & Analytics — Total Contributions KES 10,000 · Contributions Trend · Financial Health · Export buttons
Available Exports
ExportContentsFormat
PDF ReportFull summary: members, contributions, loans, financial healthPDF
Excel Export4 sheets: summary, contributions, members, loans.xlsx
Contributions CSVAll contributions with member details, dates, methodsCSV
Members CSVAll members with contribution totals and arrearsCSV
Loans CSVAll loans with repayment status and balancesCSV
Financial Health indicators The reports page shows Loan Repayment Rate, Loans vs Contributions ratio, and Active Members count as visual health meters.
Admin

Investment Portfolio

Track the chama's investment portfolio with returns, asset class breakdown, and member stake allocation.

Investment Portfolio — Portfolio value, ROI, active investments, unrealised gain, asset class breakdown

Add Investment

Click + Add Investment to record a new investment with asset class, amount, and NAV details.

Member Stakes

Click Member Stakes to view how the portfolio value is allocated across chama members.

NAV Net Asset Value — the price per investment unit. Default starting NAV is KES 100.00. Available on Premium and Pro plans.
Billing

Pricing Plans

ChamaPro offers three plans. The Free plan is a great starting point; upgrade to Premium or Pro as your chama grows.

Upgrade — Free KES 0/mo · Premium KES 999/mo (Most Popular) · Pro KES 2,499/mo · Monthly/Annual toggle
Plans — Full Feature Comparison: Members & Groups · Contributions & Finance sections
Plans — Feature Comparison: Communication · Reports & Exports · Support · Custom branding

KES 0/mo

Pro

  • SLA support

Technical Reference

URL Reference

All routes available in ChamaPro. Replace <id> with the chama's numeric ID.

Auth & General Routes
GET/POST/signup/
Create a new account
GET/POST/login/
Login with email, phone, or username
GET/logout/
Log out and redirect to login
GET/dashboard/
Main dashboard
GET/dashboard/switch/<chama_id>/
Switch active chama
GET/POST/profile/
View your profile
GET/POST/profile/edit/
Edit your profile & KYC details
Chama & Finance Routes
GET/POST/chama/create/
Create a new chama
GET/chama/<id>/members/
View members list
POST/chama/<id>/invite/
Invite a member by email
GET/POST/chama/<id>/settings/
Chama settings (admin)
GET/chama/<id>/contributions/
Contributions list
POST/chama/<id>/contributions/add/
Record contribution
GET/chama/<id>/loans/
Loans list
POST/chama/<id>/loans/apply/
Apply for loan
POST/chama/<id>/loans/<loan_id>/approve/
Approve or reject loan
GET/chama/<id>/fines/
Fines list
POST/chama/<id>/fines/add/
Issue a fine
GET/chama/<id>/reports/
Financial reports & analytics
GET/chama/<id>/export/pdf/
Download PDF report
GET/chama/<id>/export/excel/
Download Excel report
GET/wallets/
E-Wallet (personal)
GET/wallets/deposits/
Deposits page
GET/wallets/withdrawals/
Withdrawals page
M-Pesa Routes
POST/chama/<id>/mpesa/push/
Send STK Push to member's phone
POST/chama/<id>/mpesa/query/
Poll payment status
GET/chama/<id>/mpesa/transactions/
View all M-Pesa transactions
POST/mpesa/callback/
Safaricom callback endpoint (do not call directly)
Technical Reference

Data Models

Core database models and their key fields. All models are in chamapro/models.py.

ModelKey FieldsNotes
Useremail, phone, national_id, kra_pin, kyc_status, avatar, mpesa_numberExtends Django AbstractUser
Chamaname, currency, contribution_amount, contribution_day, meeting_day, is_activeis_active=False = archived
Membershipchama, user, role, joined_at, activeRoles: admin, treasurer, secretary, member
Contributionchama, member, amount, payment_method, status, dateStatuses: pending, confirmed, rejected
Loanchama, member, amount, interest_rate, term_months, status, due_dateAuto-calculates interest & balance
LoanRepaymentloan, amount, payment_method, date, statusLoan marked repaid when balance = 0
Penaltychama, member, amount, reason, status, issued_byStatuses: unpaid, paid, waived
MpesaTransactionchama, member, phone, amount, checkout_request_id, status, mpesa_receiptLinks to Contribution or LoanRepayment
Walletchama, member, balance, total_topped_up, total_contributedPersonal wallet per member per chama
WalletTransactionwallet, type, amount, reference, status, dateTypes: deposit, withdrawal, transfer
Technical Reference

M-Pesa Integration

ChamaPro uses the Safaricom Daraja API (STK Push / LIPA NA M-PESA ONLINE). Configuration is via environment variables.

Environment Variables
MPESA_ENV = sandbox or production
MPESA_CONSUMER_KEY = your_key
MPESA_CONSUMER_SECRET = your_secret
MPESA_SHORTCODE = 174379 (sandbox default)
MPESA_PASSKEY = your_passkey
MPESA_CALLBACK_URL = https://your-domain.com/mpesa/callback/
Callback URL must be public Safaricom cannot reach localhost. Use ngrok (ngrok http 8000) in development or a live server in production.
Payment Flow
1

POST /mpesa/push/

Frontend sends phone + amount. ChamaPro calls mpesa.stk_push() and creates a MpesaTransaction with status pending.

2

POST /mpesa/query/

Frontend polls every few seconds with checkout_request_id. ChamaPro checks the local DB first, then queries Safaricom if still pending.

3

POST /mpesa/callback/ (from Safaricom)

Safaricom calls this automatically with the result. ChamaPro updates the transaction status, creates a Contribution or LoanRepayment, and stores the M-Pesa receipt number.

Reference

Glossary

Key terms used throughout ChamaPro.

Chama
A Kenyan savings and investment group where members pool money regularly to support each other financially.
STK Push
SIM Toolkit Push — a payment prompt sent to a mobile phone via M-Pesa requiring the user to enter their PIN to authorize a payment.
Contribution Day
The day of the month (1–28) by which members must make their regular contribution.
Arrears
The amount a member still owes relative to their expected total contributions.
KYC
Know Your Customer — identity verification using National ID and KRA PIN. Improves credit score and loan eligibility.
Credit Score
A number between 0 and 850 calculated from contribution consistency, outstanding fines, and number of active chamas.
NAV
Net Asset Value — the price per investment unit in the Investments module. Default starting NAV is KES 100.00.
Soft Delete / Archive
Freezing a chama rather than permanently deleting it. Activity is paused and data is preserved.
Daraja API
Safaricom's developer API for integrating M-Pesa payments into applications. ChamaPro uses the LIPA NA M-PESA ONLINE (STK Push) service.
Merry-Go-Round
A rotating savings scheme where each member takes turns receiving the pooled contributions. Available on the Pro plan.
ChamaPro
